steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: Count the relevant faces, dimensions, or scale factor before setting up equations. Lengths scale by $k$, areas by $k^2$, and volumes by $k^3$; for boxes and prisms, write the needed face or volume formula directly.

steps.title: Use the area scale factor for similar figures
steps.content: When linear dimensions scale by a factor of $k$, areas scale by $k^2$. Since the side of square M is 9 times the side of square N, the area of square M is $9^2 = 81$ times the area of square N.

steps.title: Calculate the area of square M
steps.content:
Since the area of square N is 25 square centimeters:
$$\text{Area}_M = 81 \times 25 = 2,025$$
A is correct.
